subject: Jeff Bezos 的 Prometheus
object: 120 亿美元，打造面向物理世界的"人工通用工程师"
source_quote: Jeff Bezos’s Prometheus raises $12B to build an ‘artificial general engineer’ for the physical world

subject: Jeff Bezos 的 Prometheus
object: 120 亿美元，打造面向物理世界的"人工通用工程师"
source_quote: Prometheus, the physical AI startup co-founded by Jeff Bezos and Vik Bajaj, the former co-founder of Verily, Google’s life sciences unit, announced it raised $12 billion at a $41 billion valuation.

subject: Jeff Bezos 的 Prometheus
object: 120 亿美元，打造面向物理世界的"人工通用工程师"
source_quote: This is the second fundraise round for Prometheus, which launched late last year with an initial raise of $6.2 billion, according to CNBC .

subject: F2
object: $24M to Build AI for Private Credit
source_quote: Compounding Intelligence for the next era of private markets

subject: F2
object: $24M to Build AI for Private Credit
source_quote: Historically, every new private credit deal has started from scratch , but it doesn’t have to be that way. You’ve already underwritten deals with comparable structures, sponsors, and covenant packages, and the diligence work is sitting in an old memo somewhere . The hard part is finding it and making it usable (a task that LLMs struggle with as context grows).

subject: F2
object: $24M to Build AI for Private Credit
source_quote: I started F2 to close this gap . For private credit firms and banks, a lender's experience is its competitive advantage, and for most firms it's scattered across old memos, lost models, and former colleagues who have since moved on. When you can structure and reference your firm’s experience, every underwrite gets faster , and every analyst gains the pattern recognition of your most experienced MD.

subject: F2
object: $24M to Build AI for Private Credit
source_quote: And the advantage compounds . For most of private credit's history, a firm's institutional knowledge was erased with every hire, every resignation, and every deal closed and filed away. For the first time, it can compound instead — and the firms that build their own experience into how they work will hold an edge that's impossible to replicate.

subject: AWS
object: AWS Agent Marketplace: What's Coming and Why It Matters
source_quote: This isn’t just a new product. It’s a signal that the agentic AI era is no longer theoretical, it’s happening now.

subject: AWS
object: AWS Agent Marketplace: What's Coming and Why It Matters
source_quote: AWS Agent Marketplace: What’s Coming and Why It Matters

subject: AWS
object: AWS Agent Marketplace: What's Coming and Why It Matters
source_quote: Amazon Web Services is about to make a major move in the AI space. On July 15, 2025, AWS is set to officially launch its AI Agent Marketplace—a new hub where businesses can browse, buy, and deploy AI agents tailored to specific tasks, roles, and use cases. Think of it as an app store for intelligent software agents that can automate tasks, answer questions, and interact with users in real time, all built on AWS infrastructure.

subject: AWS
object: AWS Agent Marketplace: What's Coming and Why It Matters
source_quote: The AWS Agent Marketplace will feature AI agents built on Amazon’s Bedrock platform and integrated with its voice tools like Amazon Polly and Transcribe. The marketplace allows startups and developers to list their agents, offering everything from scheduling assistants to customer support bots, report generators, and industry-specific copilots.

subject: Poetic
object: $50M Series A to Automate the World's Most Complex Enterprise Processes with Reliable AI
source_quote: SAN FRANCISCO , June 10, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Today, Poetic (formerly known as Forge), the company building a new class of software that learns like AI but runs like code, announced that it has raised a $50M Series A at a $500M valuation led by Kleiner Perkins. Founders Fund, First Harmonic and OpenAI also participated in the round.

subject: Poetic
object: $50M Series A to Automate the World's Most Complex Enterprise Processes with Reliable AI
source_quote: "Markie is one of the most prescient founders I've encountered on AI, and I've had a front row seat since the beginning," said Leigh Marie Braswell, Partner at Kleiner Perkins who led the Series A. "What Poetic has built is genuinely different - a platform that can execute the complex, high-stakes processes that large enterprises actually run, with accuracy that exceeds what human teams can deliver. The fact that they were able to automate at the largest companies with the highest requirements is a reflection of how deeply the product works. And they've done so with one of the strongest teams - leaders from Palantir, UiPath, Ramp, Scale, Retool, and many more."

subject: Poetic
object: $50M Series A to Automate the World's Most Complex Enterprise Processes with Reliable AI
source_quote: New funding led by Kleiner Perkins accelerates Poetic's mission to automate complex, high-stakes business processes at super-human quality and reliability as compared to traditional AI agents

subject: Poetic
object: $50M Series A to Automate the World's Most Complex Enterprise Processes with Reliable AI
source_quote: The industries that power our lives, from healthcare to banking to insurance, still run on decades-old processes and manual work. The underlying software that permeates them has helped, but regular software has been unable to automate the real complexities of these industries with their dynamic nature and unstructured problem spaces. For years, generative AI has promised to solve these problems, yet its impact has remained largely superficial, with arms-length integrations via LLMs that aren't built to handle mission-critical tasks.

subject: Notch
object: $30 Million to Build the AI Operating System for Regulated Industries
source_quote: We Raised $30 Million to Build the AI Operating System for Regulated Industries

subject: Notch
object: $30 Million to Build the AI Operating System for Regulated Industries
source_quote: We Raised $30 Million to Build the AI Operating System for Regulated Industries

subject: Notch
object: $30 Million to Build the AI Operating System for Regulated Industries
source_quote: Today, we are announcing that Notch has raised $30 million in a Series A funding round, bringing total funding to $45 million. The round was led by Headline, with participation from Lightspeed Venture Partners, Jibe Ventures, Illuminate Financial, and Phoenix Insurance.

subject: Notch
object: $30 Million to Build the AI Operating System for Regulated Industries
source_quote: Rafael Broshi is the CEO and Co-Founder of Notch, where he’s building the future of customer experience through AI. He’s passionate about designing systems that scale with empathy, speed, and intelligence - and believes great support shouldn’t come at the cost of great experiences.

subject: Cursor
object: Auto-review 机制：用分类器智能体动态管控智能体自主权限
source_quote: However, greater autonomy introduces security risks if agents take unintended actions. This is especially true for local agents, which often run near files, credentials, environment variables, MCP tools, and have access to production systems.

subject: Cursor
object: Auto-review 机制：用分类器智能体动态管控智能体自主权限
source_quote: This week we launched Auto-review, which makes decisions around agent autonomy behave more like a dial than a switch. The core idea is that an agent should be able to move freely when the stakes are low, but slow down when its next action crosses a meaningful boundary.

subject: Cursor
object: Auto-review 机制：用分类器智能体动态管控智能体自主权限
source_quote: We determine where an action sits along that continuum with a specialized classifier agent that reviews actions in context before they run. Building it meant turning our intuition for how agent autonomy should be governed into a working model of consequence, intent, and feedback that we could test against real agent behavior.

subject: Cursor
object: Auto-review 机制：用分类器智能体动态管控智能体自主权限
source_quote: Some blocks from the classifier become user interruptions, but globally we're seeing that only about 7% of total chats in Auto-review mode lead to at least one interruption. To put that in perspective, some enterprise customers we're working with previously saw roughly 40% of actions blocked within their organization.

subject: Cursor
object: Auto-review 实现智能体自治管控
source_quote: However, greater autonomy introduces security risks if agents take unintended actions. This is especially true for local agents, which often run near files, credentials, environment variables, MCP tools, and have access to production systems.

subject: Cursor
object: Auto-review 实现智能体自治管控
source_quote: This week we launched Auto-review, which makes decisions around agent autonomy behave more like a dial than a switch. The core idea is that an agent should be able to move freely when the stakes are low, but slow down when its next action crosses a meaningful boundary.

subject: Cursor
object: Auto-review 实现智能体自治管控
source_quote: We determine where an action sits along that continuum with a specialized classifier agent that reviews actions in context before they run. Building it meant turning our intuition for how agent autonomy should be governed into a working model of consequence, intent, and feedback that we could test against real agent behavior.

subject: Cursor
object: Auto-review 实现智能体自治管控
source_quote: Some blocks from the classifier become user interruptions, but globally we're seeing that only about 7% of total chats in Auto-review mode lead to at least one interruption. To put that in perspective, some enterprise customers we're working with previously saw roughly 40% of actions blocked within their organization.

subject: WPS 笔记
object: ：AI 原生多模态，重新定义个人知识管理
source_quote: 它还支持梯形校正能力，上传了一张此前在发布会上斜侧角度拍摄的 PPT 照片，系统自动完成了梯形校正，图标、Logo，以及文字信息也完整提取了出来。

subject: Cohere
object: ，3B 活跃参数，单卡 H100 运行，支持 256K 上下文
source_quote: This week, Cohere AI team shipped its first developer-facing coding model named ‘ North Mini Code ‘. ‘North Mini Code’ is open-weight and focused at software engineers. It is a mixture-of-experts (MoE) model with 30B total parameters. Only 3B of those parameters activate per token.

subject: Cohere
object: ，3B 活跃参数，单卡 H100 运行，支持 256K 上下文
source_quote: The release is positioned around “sovereign” AI. The idea is simple: run capable models on your own terms. Small, efficient coding models let teams self-host without large GPU clusters. North Mini Code targets that gap directly.

subject: Cohere
object: ，3B 活跃参数，单卡 H100 运行，支持 256K 上下文
source_quote: North Mini Code is a 30B-A3B parameter model. The A3B stands for three billion active parameters per forward pass. Cohere optimized it for three jobs: code generation, agentic software engineering, and terminal tasks . The model is text-in, text-out. There is no image or video input.

subject: Cohere
object: ，3B 活跃参数，单卡 H100 运行，支持 256K 上下文
source_quote: The context window is 256K tokens. Maximum output length is 64K tokens. Cohere lists a minimum hardware bar of one H100 at FP8. Weights ship under Apache 2.0 on Hugging Face. You can also reach it through the Cohere API, Model Vault, and OpenRouter.

subject: xAI
object: Grok Build 插件市场，首发集成 MongoDB、Vercel、Sentry、Chrome DevTools、Cloudflare 等插件
source_quote: Grok Build is xAI’s coding agent and CLI for software engineering work. The marketplace adds a discovery and distribution layer on top. Before this, developers wired up each integration one at a time. Now a single command pulls a complete bundle into the agent. The catalog lives in the open repo xai-org/plugin-marketplace on GitHub. That repo is an index. It points at plugin sources so Grok Build can fetch them.

subject: xAI
object: Grok Build 插件市场，首发集成 MongoDB、Vercel、Sentry、Chrome DevTools、Cloudflare 等插件
source_quote: xAI launched the Grok Build Plugin Marketplace on June 11, 2026, built into the terminal.
